name: Julian's Cheese Manicotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. To make the sauce, gently sauté the onion and garlic in olive oil over medium heat until translucent (about five minutes). Add tomato paste and stir. Slowly add water and stir. Add seasonings and let simmer while preparing manicotti and filling.
3. Manicotti: Bring a large pot of water to boil, add olive oil, and drop in manicotti. Cook al dente for about 12 minutes, then drain and refresh under cold running water. Set aside.
4. Filling: In a large bowl, beat ricotta with eggs until combined. Add grated cheeses, softened butter, salt, pepper, and basil. Beat on low just until everything is combined. Gently fill cooked manicotti with cheese mixture and arrange in two greased 9" x 13" pans. Cover pasta in each pan with half the sauce; sprinkle additional Parmesan.
5. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until cheese is thoroughly melted and sauce is bubbling.
